title: Prepare Tris Buffer for Meat identification Protocol
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bb010","text":"Make sure you adjust the pH to the same temperature for the reaction in which the buffer is used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6q0ii","text":"reagents: Tris, HCl (12.1M), dd-di-DNAase free water. Note: the dilution of HCL can also be 1M.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dubuf","text":"devices: scale, thermometer,  tube rack,  pipette rack, pen. optional: incubator, magnetic stirrer or glass rod for stirring, pipettes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44e0r","text":"labware: 50 ml beaker large enough to permit pH meter measurements","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ggmj","text":"consumables: 50 ml (large) falcon tube","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Measure and add 0.24 g of Tris Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #TB0196 to the beaker. Calculation for Tris Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #TB0196 weight: MW for TRIS (C4H11NO3 ) 121.14 mol/Liter 0.04 M TRIS TRIS : m=CMV =0.04mol/L*121.14g/mol *50 ml=0.24 g of TRIS https://www.cytographica.com/lab/HHTris.html
Add 25 mL of de-ionized DNase free water to a 50 ml beaker.
Store the falcon tube in the fridge for max 4 months.
A dd water to bring the total to 50 mL .
Heat the beaker to 25 °C Celsius and maintain . This is for accurate pH measurement. Can use a water bath or an incubator to maintain the temp to 25 Celsius for example.

Add about 100 µL or less HCl 12.1 Molarity (M) Hydrochloric Acid Bioshop Catalog #HCL333.500 then iteratively measure pH and add 1 to 2μl HCL until pH decreases to pH 7.75 . The calculation below indicates that about 116uL HCl should achieve the desired pH. https://www.cytographica.com/lab/HHTris.html !! All this steps are done at the desired temperature set in the previous step. That is required because any pH measurement has to be done at a set temperature as pH is temperature dependent. The pH indicated in the referenced paper is pH 7.75 compared to pH 7 in the main paper. The initial pH should be high around 10 or 11 as Tris is alkaline and should decrease with the addition of HCl.
Transfer from beaker to a new 50 ml falcon tube labeled Tris Buffer for storage.
creator: openbioscience Adrian
